kid_mode.scale: text-4xl for main goals, text-2xl for task titles

parent_mode.body: Nunito, sans-serif (To maintain family brand consistency)
parent_mode.scale: text-3xl for dashboard headers, text-sm for labels

visual enhancers.kid_mode: Neumorphism & 'Bouncy' solid colors. Heavy use of rounded corners (rounded-3xl). No sharp edges. Colorful icons.
visual enhancers.parent_mode: Glassmorphism for sticky navs, subtle 1px borders (border-slate-200), soft shadows for dashboard cards. Clean bento-box style grids.

grids.kid_mode: Chunky list view with gap-6. Avoid dense grids. Use large touch-friendly targets.
grids.parent_mode: Bento Grid Mode B (High Density Grid for Dashboards). grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 with gap-6.

layouts.overall: Mobile-first approach. Parent dashboard optimized for desktop and mobile, Kid view heavily optimized for tablets and mobile.
layouts.kid_view: Bottom navigation for kids (Tasks, Rewards, Profile). Large hero section showing current points and avatar.
layouts.parent_view: Sidebar navigation on desktop, hamburger on mobile. Overview at the top, followed by individual child breakdowns.

motion.kid_mode: Bouncy entrance animations (spring physics), confetti on task completion, coin drop animations for rewards.
motion.parent_mode: Subtle fades, slide-in panels for task creation, smooth accordion down for chore details.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text over vibrant backgrounds meets APCA standards. Use dark text (e.g., #1E293B) on yellow/blue backgrounds.
accessibility.touch_targets: Minimum 48x48px touch targets for kid mode.
accessibility.motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion, disable confetti if enabled.

media.icons: lucide-react (Use high stroke-width, e.g., 2.5 for kid mode to make them look friendlier)
media.illustrations: Use 3D style elements and large colorful emojis for rewards/tasks if images aren't available.

3d libraries.animations: framer-motion (for spring animations, layout transitions between parent/kid mode)

image_urls.description: Used in the kid profile selection and header
Used in the kid profile selection and header
Used as a visual for the rewards section or points bank
Used in the parent login/registration screen to convey trust and family connection

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a hybrid full-stack application with TWO distinct visual modes: a playful Kid View and an approachable Parent Dashboard.
Do NOT build a generic SaaS dashboard for the Kid View. It must feel like a game. Use big rounded corners (rounded-3xl), bright colors, large touch targets, and bouncy animations.
Parent View should use a clean Bento Box grid style, soft pastels, and clear data visualization to avoid overwhelming the parent.
Implement a 'PIN Gate' modal to switch from Kid Mode to Parent Mode.
Use framer-motion for smooth transitions and 'react-confetti' for celebrating completed tasks.
Ensure no third-party builder watermark or branding remains anywhere in the app.
Include data-testid attributes on all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, task items) for testing.
Use lucide-react for icons, scaling them up (w-8 h-8 or w-10 h-10) for the kid interface.
CRITICAL: You have .js files not .tsx file, so build all components as .jsx or .js, do not use typescript.
Install and add lucide-react, framer-motion, and react-confetti via npm/yarn.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: Avoid the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Do not use generic centered layouts or simplistic gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and soft shadows.
Never apply universal transitions (transition: all). Target specific properties like transform and opacity.
DO NOT use transparent backgrounds for headers or dropdowns where content can scroll underneath.
Always use modern pill-shaped or fully rounded buttons for kids' apps, with active/press states (e.g., scale-95).
Do NOT use emojis as UI icons. Use a professional icon set like lucide-react.
Use 2-3x more spacing than typical apps. The Parent dashboard needs breathing room, and Kid mode needs massive touch targets.
Use sonner for all toast notifications.
